title: On systemd pod start: conmon.pid O_PATH file descriptor to proper file descriptor: Permission denied
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I am currently experimenting with podman generate systemd . My target is to build unit files out of kube pod definitions.
Unfortunately I am getting the following error message:

```
Jun 06 16:08:59 cow01.priv systemd[1]: Starting Podman pod-nginx.service...
Jun 06 16:08:59 cow01.priv podman[698502]: b37313abad5f-infra
Jun 06 16:08:59 cow01.priv systemd[1]: pod-nginx.service: Can't convert PID files /secure/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/add029ed9dfcc76f48a9b5144664c002f017c5b9e31f3854cf0fb440c1e0fbb9/userdata/conmon.pid O_PATH file descriptor to proper file descriptor: Permission denied
Jun 06 16:08:59 cow01.priv systemd[1]: pod-nginx.service: Can't convert PID files /secure/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/add029ed9dfcc76f48a9b5144664c002f017c5b9e31f3854cf0fb440c1e0fbb9/userdata/conmon.pid O_PATH file descriptor to proper file descriptor: Permission denied
```

The podman version is `4.0.2` and running in `CentOS 8 Stream`. To get there I did the following steps:


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Given the pod file (as `nginx.yaml`):

```
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-4.0.2
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2022-06-06T14:06:23Z"
  labels:
    app: nginx
  name: nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - args:
    - nginx
    - -g
    - daemon off;
    image: docker.io/library/nginx:latest
    name: mynginx
    resources: {}
    securityContext:
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - CAP_MKNOD
        - CAP_AUDIT_WRITE
  restartPolicy: Never
status: {}
```

1. `podman play kube nginx.yaml`
2. `podman generate systemd -f -n nginx`
3. `systemctl daemon-reload`
4. `systemctl start pod-nginx`

**Describe the results you received:**


`systemctl status pod-nginx` -> the error message above pops up and the pod does not start

I did disable SELinux for a test - but it did not help.


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.17.7

Built:      Tue Mar 15 20:15:06 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

```
# rpm -q podman
podman-4.0.2-1.module_el8.7.0+1106+45480ee0.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes (latest available under CentOS 8 Stream.

comments.body:
There have been fixes to support kubernetes.yaml files in systemd unit files.
@vrothberg PTAL

Thanks for reaching out, @tvannahl.

The issue is that the pod is currently running but was not started via `systemctl` but via `podman play kube`.  If you do `systemctl stop` and then a `systemctl start`, it will work as expected.

As Dan mentioned, Podman v4.2 will ship with a new way of running K8s yaml in systemd. We'll prepare a blog for that around the time of the release.
